title: Reverse-engineering ned.exe — Discord help from 'Ethanol' on PyInstaller extract + uncompyle6
html:
Discord (or class chat) message from classmate Ethanol at 6:54 pm walking Yahir through reversing the ned.exe sample. Discovery: the binary is packed Python — strings like Py_DecRef give it away, so a pyinstxtractor → uncompyle6 flow can recover source. Three links: netscylla.com walkthrough on reversing Python EXEs, the uncompyle6 PyPI page (since decompyle3 doesn't handle Python 2.7), and the pyinstxtractor-web Netlify tool that takes the EXE and returns a zip.

TL;DR

  • ned.exe is a PyInstaller-packed Python binary — confirmed via Py_DecRef symbol leakage.
  • Reverse-engineering pipeline: pyinstxtractor.py → extract pyc → uncompyle6 → original source.
  • Ethanol got this from office hours; passing it forward to Yahir saved him the office-hours trip.

malware analysis lab · network forensics · python · osu
